single kpi is weak without consulting your leadership or even informing them their technology consulting strategy is to simply buy as many smaller consulting companies as they can and throw them all together and hope they somehow produce revenue it's all a mess with goals constantly shifting new nonsense initiatives rolled out every quarter and constantly changing goalposts they basically said if you want to get promoted you have to basically be doing that job for a year it's wild there is a constant push to be contributing to 'the business' and internal projects even if you are fully staffed and utilized doing your client work is considered the bare minimum and will likely get you passed over for promotions or maybe even fired but if you do this extra credit you had better make sure your utilization numbers are still good they also don't like it when you are overutilized they want it both ways most of the front line workers were great and a pleasure tow work with but managers tended to be duplicitous and two-faced everyone is there to save their own skin and won't hesitate to pass the blame to you warranted or not i joined a project that had been going for three years in a role completely unfamiliar to me despite being very clear about this my feedback provider still gave me a negative review on my feedback cycle a month later apparently i was not allowed any time to grow into the role i was there for 2 years and saw three rounds of layoffs the third round they didn't even bother announcing not a healthy company if you ask me just too big too fail ey was also supposed to do a big split between their accounting and consulting businesses at the last second the american partners rebelled and all that work had to be thrown away for some reason bonuses were weak that year can't figure it out but seriously company is a mess and the firm structure makes it so that there are way too many cooks in the kitchen and there is no real clear direction for anything -lots of layoffs -weak bonuses and raises while i was there -lots of layers of management who are all not very good -pay is not great though they'll tell you its above average -bad management -constantly changing goalposts -unorganized -ultra competitive -work can be demanding -managers will bend over backwards for clients at expense of team
